steps.title: Set up the equal-ratio equation
steps.content:
Since both rectangles have the same length-to-width ratio:
$$\frac{12}{w} = \frac{18}{x}$$
where $x$ is the width of rectangle B.

steps.title: Solve by cross-multiplication
steps.content:
Cross multiply:
$$12x = 18w$$
Divide both sides by 12:
$$x = \frac{18w}{12} = \frac{3w}{2}$$
The width of rectangle B is $\frac{3}{2}w$. That matches Choice A — $\frac{3}{2}w$.
